selected_engineer_work.title: Real-Time Interactive Trivia System for Streaming
selected_engineer_work.content:
A production-scale real-time interactive system designed for synchronized second-screen participation during live sports broadcasts.

The platform combines a Remotion-based rendering pipeline that dynamically generates trivia video segments with a mobile-first web application where viewers participate in synchronized live quiz sessions.

The backend integrates with the streaming environment through WebSockets to coordinate game state synchronization, player sessions, leaderboards, and real-time gameplay interactions across the broadcast and connected devices.

selected_engineer_work.title: Media Rendering Workflow System
selected_engineer_work.content:
A monorepo-based media rendering workflow system designed to orchestrate asynchronous video generation pipelines using Remotion and Node.js.

The platform exposes backend endpoints that enqueue rendering jobs into a MySQL-backed queue system, where a worker process continuously monitors render eligibility, processes jobs, tracks lifecycle states, and manages retry and cancellation workflows.

The system includes a Next.js operational dashboard for monitoring render progress, reviewing queued and completed jobs, and managing render operations through polling-based status synchronization. The rendering infrastructure was intentionally designed for a locally integrated operational environment as part of a larger internal platform ecosystem.

selected_engineer_work.title: SPA Prototype With jQuery
selected_engineer_work.content:
Architectural SPA prototype built with jQuery to explore component-driven frontend architecture, modular application structure, and maintainable client-side systems without relying on modern frontend frameworks.

The project includes client-side routing, authentication flows, reusable UI modules, and dashboard state management implemented in a low-dependency environment. This exploration reflects my interest in scalable frontend architecture and adaptability across both modern and legacy ecosystems.
